metric_value: Delivering SSO since 1984; focus on Unix then client-server then Internet
notes: DynaSoft described as 14-year veteran of security solutions expanding from Unix to client-server to Internet SSO

metric_value: Works directly with customers to fold lessons into product
notes: Aberdeen describes DynaSoft as small enough to work directly with users and integrate learnings into releases

metric_value: HP chose BoKS technology for HP Praesidium Single Sign-On
notes: Strategic endorsement: Hewlett-Packard incorporated DynaSoft security technology into Praesidium SSO

metric_value: Sun chose DynaSoft as strategic partner for Sun Security Manager
notes: Strategic endorsement: Sun Microsystems chose DynaSoft for technology and products in Sun Security Manager

metric_value: IS finding slide-ware instead of demonstrable product and reference accounts
notes: Aberdeen field research: IS decision makers report SSO 'appears to be out of reach' due to lack of real products

metric_value: Non-interoperable user profile databases across unlike platforms
notes: Key SSO barrier: disparate platform user profiles creating high IS management expense

metric_value: Common authentication authorization and auditing across multiple technology domains
notes: First SSO requirement from IS veterans: common AAA functions across all platforms

metric_value: Reusable framework for evolving business needs
notes: Second SSO requirement: must support business change over time without IT staff expertise overload

metric_value: No cadre of highly-skilled IS staff required
notes: Third SSO requirement: must be manageable by moderate-skilled administrators

metric_value: Full project deployments from start to finish in less than 60 days
notes: User-reported benchmark: BoKS deployments completing in under 60 days per Aberdeen user research

metric_value: Moderate-skilled admins managing hundreds to thousands of nodes and tens of thousands of users
notes: User-reported scalability: low-to-moderate skilled admins handling large deployments

metric_value: No impact on existing applications and networks
notes: Most consistent user finding: zero impact on existing apps during BoKS deployment

metric_value: RSA-based cryptography for in-transit information protection in SSSO
notes: BoKS SSSO uses RSA cryptography by default; modular engine can be swapped for export compliance

metric_value: X.509 certificates supported as credential type in BoKS PSDs
notes: BoKS Desktop supports X.509 certificates alongside ISO smart cards and software PSDs

metric_value: Entrust Technologies CA services integrate with BoKS for multi-level authorizations
notes: Large enterprises using BoKS with Entrust CA for supplier/partner/customer authorizations

metric_value: BankBoston, BP Oil, Citibank, Chase Manhattan, Indian Telecom, Mannesmann Mobilfunk, Merrill Lynch, SmithKline Beecham, Telecom Italia, Telmex, Telstra
notes: Named enterprise users representing global financial and telecom sectors

metric_value: Aberdeen recommends standardizing on DynaSoft and BoKS
notes: Aberdeen conclusion: 'you cannot go wrong by standardizing on DynaSoft and BoKS'

metric_value: Validated: acquired by Security Dynamics for $115M within months
notes: Security Dynamics acquired DynaSoft for $115M in July 1997 (CNET Dec 1997; RSA Security Wikipedia); confirmed Aberdeen's strong positive assessment

metric_value: SSO will become essential enterprise infrastructure
notes: Aberdeen frames SSO as critical for managing geographically dispersed enterprises with multiple platforms

metric_value: Confirmed: SSO is universal enterprise infrastructure
notes: SSO became foundational enterprise architecture via SAML (2005) OAuth (2006) OIDC (2014); Okta IPO 2017 at $2.1B; Microsoft Active Directory SSO ubiquitous

metric_value: NT Server version in field test; planned for future release
notes: Gap in 1997: BoKS Manager for Windows NT Server not yet released; planned

metric_value: Users consistently give DynaSoft high marks for helping deploy projects ahead of schedule
notes: Aberdeen user research finding: DynaSoft's customer success focus is key differentiator

metric_value: HP and Sun endorsements consolidate support, widen distribution, add professional services
notes: Aberdeen analysis: HP and Sun 'harvesting BoKS for their own security programs' validates enterprise fitness

metric_value: Software credential containers independent of specific credential implementations
notes: BoKS PSD design allows swappable credential types: RSA keys smart cards digital signatures X.509

metric_value: Security Dynamics built security portfolio via acquisitions (RSA Data Security 1996 DynaSoft 1997)
notes: Security Dynamics acquired RSA Data Security in July 1996 for ~$251M then DynaSoft in July 1997 for $115M; renamed RSA Security in 1999
